_extracted.professional_summary: Experienced international development professional with deep expertise in Women, Peace, and Security (WPS) spanning grassroots implementation, research, and policy advocacy. Demonstrates commitment to advancing sustainable development and empowering marginalized communities through collaboration with international organizations, with specialized focus on gender equality, conflict resolution, economic inclusion, and educational opportunities for minorities. Published researcher and consultant with proven experience advising high-level government officials and leading NGO operations in complex humanitarian contexts.

employment.description_of_duties: Directed the organisation's efforts to combat gender-based violence in the Afar region of Ethiopia through programmes that promote girls' enrolment in education and post-secondary pathways, as well as initiatives advancing women's financial inclusion by establishing a women's cooperatives. In the challenging context of the civil war of Ethiopia, I pivoted towards addressing the gendered impacts of conflict and led war emergency response efforts including the delivery of essential medical equipment to those heavily affected by war.

Founded Lem Chaka, a company that promotes circular economy through the recycling of food waste sourced from hotels in Addis Ababa to produce high-quality organic fertilizers. Led a team of 12 people to establish the company from the ground up. Ensured that each initiative went beyond environmental responsibility; and tackled extreme poverty and the inclusion of the most vulnerable groups in every project. Recipient of a $246,000 grant from USAID for the implementation of CapEx equipments.

employment.employer: Office of the President of Ethiopia
employment.description_of_duties: Collaborated with and reported to H.E. Sahle-Work Zewde, during her tenure as President of Ethiopia, to provide strategic guidance and develop key policy recommendation on gender equality during high-level international conferences. Conferences attended: Global Women Leaders High-Level Dialogue (Madrid, January 2024), High-Level Dialogue on Women in Conflicts in the Great Lake Region (Luanda, October 2024), High-Level Forum on the Future of Africa-Europe Relations (Brussels, November 2024).

employment.employer: African Women Peace and Security Institute
employment.description_of_duties: Gender consultant at the African Women Peace and Security Institute (AWPSI). I fulfilled various roles including research, coordination, and logistical support to build the AWPSI. Led research totalling over 140 pages involving a comparative analysis on the implementation of peace agreements, some spearheaded by women, in conflict-affected nations. Analysed and drew insights from the gendered impacts of conflicts in Afghanistan, Colombia, Liberia, and the DRC resulting in a roadmap proposal for post-conflict Ethiopia.

Consultant at the United Nations Economic Commission for Africa in the Gender, Poverty, and Social Policy (GPSP) Division, focusing on extreme poverty and gender equality. Supported the Chief of the GPSP division in the drafting and preparation of meetings, external presentations and technical proposals.

Consultant at the Office of the United Nations High Commissioner for Human Rights in the Eastern Regional Office (OHCHR-EARO). Co-headed missions related to Djibouti. Fostered collaborations with Civil Society Organisations (CSOs) in Djibouti, engaged in exchanges to empower them, conducted trainings and collectively addressed the challenges they encountered. Contributed to the communications team responsible for weekly reporting to headquarters in Geneva.

_extracted.professional_summary: As a Digital Financial Services (DFS) IT Manager with over a decade of progressive experience in IT infrastructure, systems administration, and financial technology, Abebaw Belay Marye brings a multifaceted blend of technical proficiency, industry knowledge, and managerial skills to drive digital transformation of financial services. Currently serving as DFS-IT Manager at Ethiopost, he oversees digital financial services operations ensuring delivery of secure, efficient, and customer-centric digital banking solutions. With a strong background as Senior System Administrator at EthSwitch and System Engineer at Kifiya Financial Technology, he demonstrates expertise in IT infrastructure design, systems administration, cybersecurity, project management, and fintech innovations. His qualifications include a Master's degree in Computer Engineering, advanced certifications in digital finance and project management, and proven leadership in managing technical teams and implementing enterprise IT solutions.

employment.description_of_duties: Develop and implement digital financial products and services, ensuring alignment with business objectives and customer needs. Stay abreast of fintech trends and innovations, integrating new technologies to enhance digital financial services offerings. Ensure regulatory compliance and adherence to financial industry standards in all digital financial services operations. Lead strategic planning efforts for digital financial services, including market analysis, product design, and rollout strategies. Manage partnerships with fintech companies, mobile money operators, financial institutions, and stakeholders to expand digital service offerings. Oversee risk management processes to identify and mitigate cybersecurity threats, fraud risks, and data security issues. Provide leadership to a team of professionals, offering guidance, mentorship, and support for achieving organizational goals. Execute project management methodologies to ensure the successful implementation of digital financial service projects.

A solutions architect that requires extensive knowledge of IT infrastructure. Develop solutions for an organization. Design and implement IT solutions for improving an organization's operations. Evaluate business needs and develop solutions to solve issues in the company. Provide recommendations to the company and stakeholders on what type of solutions would maximize their productivity or reduce issues in their operations. Collect relevant documentation to develop technical roadmaps and propose solutions. Oversee the systems administration department within an organization. Supervise Operations team, monitor system performance and configure new software and hardware. Have excellent troubleshooting skills and hands-on experience with various operating systems. Manage systems administrators who oversee day-to-day problems. This is a managerial role that requires leadership skills and demonstrated experience in systems administration. Perform cost-benefit and return-on-investment analyses for proposed systems to aid management in making implementation decisions. Interact and negotiate with vendors, outsourcers, and contractors to secure system-related products and services. Develop and implement backup and recovery strategies to protect data and ensure business continuity.

System engineer overseeing the development, installation, configuration, and administration of computing systems. Troubleshoot technical issues and develop effective solutions. Keep abreast of the latest advancements in technology, protocols, and best practices. Apply proven communication, analytical, and problem-solving skills to help identify, communicate, and resolve issues to maximize the benefit of IT systems in the organization. Ensure the stability, integrity, and efficient operation of the in-house information systems that support core organizational functions. Develop and implement backup and recovery strategies to protect data and ensure business continuity.

_extracted.professional_summary: Yesu Girma Taye is a seasoned finance professional with a distinguished career spanning the banking, digital financial services, microfinance, and agribusiness sectors. Known for his sharp analytical skills and strategic problem-solving abilities, Yesu has a proven track record of delivering tailored financial solutions that drive organizational growth and long-term sustainability. His expertise lies in blending technical finance acumen with business leadership to tackle complex challenges, enabling organizations to thrive in dynamic and often volatile markets. Over the course of his career, Yesu has successfully led high-impact investments and conducted in-depth feasibility studies across multiple industries, including agribusiness, construction, and financial services. His leadership has been instrumental in guiding organizations toward financial stability, operational efficiency, and scalable growth, particularly through innovative financing solutions. His strategic oversight and financial insight have not only unlocked new revenue streams but also enhanced organizational resilience in the face of economic challenges. One of Yesu's core strengths is his ability to manage projects that demand a deep understanding of regulatory environments, complex financial modeling, and stakeholder coordination. His approach to problem-solving is holistic—he considers financial, operational, and strategic factors to craft solutions that are both pragmatic and forward-looking. Whether establishing new microfinance institutions, securing capital for large-scale investments, or preparing businesses for investment readiness, Yesu consistently delivers measurable results. His ability to work seamlessly with international teams across diverse cultural contexts has further solidified his reputation as a global leader in financial consulting.

employment.description_of_duties: As the Project Team Leader for the Ethiopia Connect Project at AMAS, I play a pivotal role in driving strategic initiatives to enhance financial inclusion, particularly access to finance for smallholder farmers in Ethiopia. I am leading the establishment of a digital-led microfinance institution (MFI) in collaboration with both national and international consortium parties of my project by maintaining respect for cultural and individual diversities. My leadership was instrumental in raising 200% of the required capital to launch the MFI. Additionally, I manage stakeholder relationships, represent the organization in key meetings and oversee multiple high-value financial models and feasibility studies.

At Resilience BV, I spearheaded an assignment within the framework of the Ethio-Netherlands Seed Partnership project. My primary responsibility was to develop an in-depth financing strategy tailored specifically for seed-producing companies. This role entailed a thorough assessment of the financial needs and challenges faced by these companies, and devising a strategy that would enable them to access essential financial services. My approach involved identifying funding sources that would support the growth and scaling of seedling operations. By ensuring that these companies had the financial backing needed, I contributed in fostering their development and success within the agricultural sector.

As a Freelance Consultant at Precise Consult, I successfully led a significant assignment commissioned by the International Finance Corporation, which was centered around the development of an oilseed sourcing and financing strategy. I analyzed market trends, financial flows, and sourcing mechanisms to craft a comprehensive strategy that not only met the project's objectives but also provided actionable insights and recommendations. My contributions were instrumental in shaping a strategy that facilitated efficient sourcing and financing processes, thereby supporting the broader goals of the IFC's initiative. As a result, IFC can design a financial product that benefits more than 150,000 smallholder farmers.

As a Senior Agri-Finance Specialist at the Ethiopian Agricultural Transformation Agency (ATA), I led the design and implementation of innovative financial schemes to support financial institutions and Agricultural businesses. I spearheaded partnerships with different financial institutions to facilitate asset leasing, wholesale financing, and SME credit lines. My role involved managing grants, preparing budgets, and delivering comprehensive reports to stakeholders. I provided technical assistance to 35 agribusiness companies, helping them achieve investment readiness through financial management, business planning, and ESG compliance. My leadership and communication skills were key in building strong partnerships and ensuring the success of these initiatives.

employment.description_of_duties: At United Bank S.C., I progressed through various roles, ultimately serving as a Credit Analyst. In this capacity, I appraised over $10.3 million in working capital loans and financed projects worth more than $7 million by conducting thorough feasibility studies. My responsibilities included conducting market research, financial analysis, due diligence, and monitoring market trends to support potential transactions. I managed a credit portfolio of $4.67 million and handled essential trade finance documents like Letters of Credit, Cash Against Documents, and Telegraphic Transfers. I consistently demonstrated strong analytical, communication, and decision-making skills in this dynamic banking environment.

_extracted.professional_summary: Helen Asnake Mekonen is a highly experienced Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E) and programme management professional with 17+ years of expertise in international development, humanitarian relief, and management consulting. Currently serving as Monitoring and Reporting Lead for Global initiatives, she brings extensive knowledge in designing and implementing complexity-aware mixed methods M&E systems, leading M&E components of large donor-funded facilities (USAID, DFID, EU), and managing diverse stakeholder relationships across African and international contexts. With over 10 years of specialized experience coordinating M&E systems in Ethiopia and the region, Helen has demonstrated excellence in developing holistic M&E frameworks, logframes, value for money strategies, and knowledge management tools. Her strong leadership capabilities extend to building and mentoring high-performing M&E teams, creating effective networks with local and international stakeholders including Ethiopian government ministries, and delivering robust reporting and learning outputs that inform strategic decision-making.

Objective: provide flexible, demand-driven technical assistance to Government of Ethiopia (GoE) ministries, agencies, state-owned enterprises and industrial parks in order to enhance the effectiveness of public investments and improve the business enabling environment, particularly for exporters. Supported project managers in regular monitoring, report writing, communicating impact and lessons learned. Developed MEL strategy, logframe & value for money strategy & risk matrix for the programme. Set programme meeting agendas and facilitate review meetings, lessons learned meetings. Managed online database and knowledge management system of the programme, ensuring the documentation of all project outputs in the online library. Supported the development of research on gender ''Power of parity'' reports in Ethiopia and worked with other organizations to disseminate the findings of the study.

Developed the M&E, documentation and knowledge management systems of the programme in focus countries and managed the learning and M&E components of 14 projects in 10 countries in Africa, Asia and the Middle East. Developed proposals on early warning systems and public health emergency preparedness programmes and consulted more than 14 project managers in setting up M&E system and indicators to track impact. Developed a range of innovative and traditional approaches of monitoring and evaluation to support the programme in maximizing uptake and impact of programmatic evidence. Trained partners and international NGO staff on M&E and learning and managed the team of learning advisers in focus countries. Analysis of M&E data. Developed ToRs for field research, prepared case studies, blogs and articles. Conducted surveys, assessments and baselines in DRC, Kenya and Ethiopia to inform the design of preparedness programmes and to track impact of the different projects. Developed communication strategy, marketing strategy of the project website. Engaged with different stakeholders to communicate programme impact. Engaged on social media to communicate learnings (Twitter, Facebook, etc). Set up an agenda for different regional and international conferences in the area of preparedness and capacity building. Facilitated a number of high-level international and regional conferences and panel discussions on localisation, capacity building and other major humanitarian issues. Worked to facilitate collaboration in the sector between governments, bilateral, multilateral and local organizations in each country to leverage impact and sustain the impact of the programme. Engaged with donors, consultancy firms and contracting agencies.
